enclosed_documents: Forensic Causation Report with annotated photography
summary_paragraphs: Please find enclosed the complete forensic documentation package for the property at 440 Christensen Rd, Waterloo, IA 50701, USA. Our independent inspection has documented storm-related damage to the Roofing, Siding, Gutters.

The enclosed reports include annotated photography of all identified damage, an Xactimate-format replacement cost estimate, and a forensic causation analysis establishing the relationship between documented weather events and observed damage.
Please do not hesitate to contact us should you require any additional information or wish to schedule a joint inspection.

code_violations.requirement: Asphalt shingles shall be installed in accordance with this section AND the manufacturer's installation instructions — adopts manufacturer spec as code-enforceable requirement
code_violations.status: Required — manufacturer spec controls fastener count, exposure, starter, drip edge, and warranty-critical details

code_violations.requirement: Drip edge required at eaves and rake edges of shingle roofs; min 2-inch overlap at joints, min 1/4-inch projection below sheathing
Underlayment shall be applied in accordance with manufacturer's installation instructions — ASTM D226 / D4869 / D6757 labeling required
Factory starter strip course required along eaves and rakes with pre-applied sealant strip at roof edge; field-cut shingles do not qualify as starter (no sealant = wind-uplift failure)
code_violations.status: Required — included in scope per manufacturer wind-warranty spec

code_violations.requirement: Ice barrier required in areas where the average daily temperature in January is ≤25°F or where ice damming has a history; min 2 feet inside exterior wall line (Climate Zones 5A+ including OH/NY)
Flashings shall be installed at wall and roof intersections, wherever there is a change in roof slope or direction, and around roof openings
Exterior walls shall provide the building with a weather-resistant exterior wall envelope — continuous barrier, no terminations at corners or penetrations

Water-resistive barrier required behind exterior cladding; applied shingle-fashion with min 2-inch horizontal and 6-inch vertical laps; must wrap continuously around outside corners per manufacturer install spec (Tyvek/StuccoWrap min 6-inch corner overlap)
code_violations.status: Required — forces full-elevation replacement when carrier approves partial

code_violations.requirement: Wall flashing required at all exterior wall openings (windows, doors, deck ledgers, penetrations) to redirect water to the exterior of the WRB
code_violations.status: Required — window wraps and door flashings non-negotiable

code_violations.requirement: Vinyl siding shall conform to ASTM D3679 and be installed in accordance with this section AND the manufacturer's installation instructions (fastener spacing 16-inch o.c. max, 1/32-inch gap under fastener head for thermal movement, corrosion-resistant fasteners with min 3/8-inch head)
code_violations.status: Required — manufacturer install spec adopted as code

code_violations.requirement: Wood/hardboard siding shall be installed in accordance with manufacturer's installation instructions — naturally durable species or preservative-treated; corrosion-resistant fasteners with min 1.5-inch penetration into framing
code_violations.status: Required — manufacturer install spec adopted as code

conclusion_paragraphs: Based on our forensic analysis of 27 documented findings, the property at 440 Christensen Rd, Waterloo, IA 50701, USA sustained confirmed storm damage from the April 14, 2026 hail event. Chalk-tested hail impacts across all visible steep-slope shingles consistently exceed actionable density thresholds per HAAG Engineering standards, with circular fracture patterns and granule displacement exposing the underlying asphalt mat. The flat EPDM/rolled membrane section over the garage exhibits dozens of confirmed hail impact marks compromising membrane integrity, while the B-vent chimney pipe assembly and pipe boot flashings display catastrophic hail-induced fractures with failed sealant joints. Corroborating physical evidence — including granule accumulation at drip edges and window head trim, directional bruising on hip and ridge caps, and impact indentations on galvanized metal components — collectively establishes a single, large-diameter hail event as the proximate cause of damage across all roof sections and penetrations.

The confirmed functional damage to the architectural laminated composition shingles, EPDM/rolled membrane, B-vent chimney pipe assembly, and pipe boot flashings requires full roof system replacement rather than spot repair. Uniform hail strike density across all orientations and both steep and flat roof sections renders selective repair structurally and aesthetically infeasible, as undamaged material of matching profile, color, and weathering is unavailable for integration. Additionally, our inspection documented code violations under IA-IRC §R905.2.2, where multiple shingle layers visible at the drip edge mandate a full tear-off to deck upon any re-roofing, and under IA-IRC §R903.2, where the compromised weather barrier at the chimney penetration flashing must be restored to code-compliant condition during replacement.

Based on the documented damage, applicable building codes, and industry standards, our analysis identifies the need for full replacement of the steep-slope architectural shingle system on all slopes, the flat EPDM/rolled membrane over the garage, the B-vent chimney pipe assembly with associated flashing boot, all damaged pipe boot flanges, and hip and ridge cap shingles. The scope further includes tear-off to deck per IA-IRC §R905.2.2, detach-and-reset of the active satellite dish with proper flashing of mount penetrations upon reinstallation, remediation of the abandoned satellite mount bracket's unsealed penetrations, and inspection of west elevation siding for hail impact documentation as indicated by field observations. This scope determination is grounded solely in the physical evidence documented at the property and the code-triggered requirements identified during our forensic inspection.

This forensic determination was conducted in accordance with HAAG Engineering residential roof inspection standards for hail damage identification and density assessment, NRCA guidelines for roof system evaluation and repair feasibility, and the International Residential Code as adopted in Iowa (IA-IRC) for re-roofing and weather barrier requirements. These industry-recognized standards provide the technical framework for distinguishing functional storm damage from pre-existing wear, evaluating repair versus replacement thresholds, and identifying code-compliance triggers that govern the required scope of work.
forensic_findings.damage_summary: Property at 440 Christensen Rd, Waterloo, IA sustained severe hail damage from the April 14, 2026 storm event. Architectural laminated shingles across all visible slopes exhibit widespread circular hail impacts with granule displacement exposing dark asphalt mat — functional damage confirmed per HAAG standards. The flat EPDM/rolled membrane section over the garage shows dozens of circular hail impact marks. Granule debris in gutters and on window trim confirms recent storm-related granule loss. Multiple slopes show uniform hail strike density indicating full roof replacement is warranted. The shingle damage pattern is consistent across all elevations, making spot repair infeasible. Insurance claim #0824712848 filed with Allstate. Photos 6–10 document a residential asphalt shingle roof with severe hail damage across all visible slopes. The chimney/B-vent penetration shows impact indentations and deteriorated flashing with cracked sealant joints—confirmed hail fracture, not aging. Multiple roof slopes exhibit widespread granule displacement with dark mat exposure visible at standard viewing distance. Ridge vent and hip cap shingles show storm damage. The chimney pipe assembly has catastrophic hail fracture at the joint collar. Combined with contractor-noted rolled membrane damage on the garage and west-side siding damage, full roof system replacement is warranted—spot repair is infeasible given uniform hail impact distribution across all slopes and penetrations. Photos 11–15 document hail damage across multiple roof components on this asphalt shingle property. Pipe boot flashing is fractured from hail impact (catastrophic), the B-vent flashing shows impact indentations, and the satellite dish mount creates unsealed penetrations requiring detach-and-reset during re-roof. Soffit and fascia appear intact but dozens of hail impacts are confirmed on shingle field and penetration flashings, supporting full roof replacement. Comprehensive hail damage across asphalt shingle slopes and EPDM flat membrane on garage. Multiple slopes show dozens of hail impacts with granule displacement exposing dark mat — functional damage confirmed per HAAG standards. Flat EPDM membrane exhibits numerous circular hail impact depressions. Shingle edge detail at drip edge shows multiple layers and deteriorated seal line. Ridge vent area shows widespread hail strikes across both slopes. Full roof replacement required — spot repair infeasible due to pervasive hail pattern across all visible surfaces and the April 14, 2026 storm event. Photo 21 shows the roof-to-wall junction along the eave edge above a deck with a bay window below. The aluminum gutter and drip edge metal running along the eave show visible deformation consistent with hail impact. The asphalt shingle roof surface exhibits granule displacement. The gutter brackets and aluminum trim around the bay window are soft metals susceptible to hail denting. Contractor noted siding damage on the west side — this elevation shows horizontal lap siding with the bay window trim exposed to direct storm impact. Full replacement is warranted given confirmed hail indicators on soft metals and shingle surfaces.

executive_summary: On April 14, 2026, the property at 440 Christensen Rd, Waterloo, IA 50701, USA sustained damage from a hail-producing storm system. A forensic inspection was conducted and documented across 20 photographs, evaluating the architectural laminated composite shingle roof system on the primary dwelling, the flat EPDM/rolled membrane roof section over the attached garage, associated penetration flashings, soft-metal gutters and trim, and exterior siding components. This report establishes causation and documents the scope of hail damage to the property's roofing and exterior systems.

The inspection confirmed pervasive hail damage across all visible roof slopes and the garage membrane. Architectural laminated shingles exhibit widespread circular hail impacts with granule displacement exposing the dark asphalt mat beneath — functional damage confirmed per HAAG Engineering standards. The flat EPDM membrane section displays dozens of circular hail impact depressions consistent with the same storm event. Soft-metal components — including aluminum gutters, drip edge, and trim — show deformation consistent with hail strike patterns, while penetration flashings at the chimney B-vent collar and pipe boots exhibit catastrophic hail fracture with cracked sealant joints confirmed as impact-induced rather than age-related deterioration.

The uniform distribution of hail impacts across all roof slopes, penetration flashings, and the EPDM membrane renders spot repair technically infeasible; selective shingle replacement cannot restore system integrity when damage density is pervasive and replacement materials cannot match the weathered profile, granule color, or dimensional characteristics of the existing aged shingles. Additionally, any re-roofing activity on this structure triggers compliance with the IRC as adopted in Iowa, including current underlayment, flashing, and fastening requirements, necessitating a full system approach. The catastrophic fracture of the pipe boot and chimney collar flashings independently compromises the roof's weather-tight envelope, further confirming that full roof system replacement — inclusive of the shingle field, EPDM membrane, all penetration flashings, and damaged soft-metal components — is the only code-compliant and manufacturer-consistent repair path.
key_arguments: Architectural shingles on steep slopes show dozens of circular hail impacts per test-square-equivalent area — functional damage confirmed requiring full replacement per HAAG standards.
Flat EPDM/rolled membrane over garage exhibits dozens of circular hail impact marks across full surface — membrane integrity compromised.
Granule accumulation at drip edge and window head trim confirms active storm-related granule loss from April 14, 2026 event.
Damage is uniform across all visible slopes and both steep and flat roof sections — spot repair is not feasible; full roof system replacement warranted.
Contractor context notes siding damage on west side — west elevation siding inspection recommended for hail impact documentation.

B-vent chimney pipe (photos 6 & 10) shows hail impact indentations at joint collar with fractured sealant—catastrophic penetration failure requiring full replacement of pipe assembly and flashing boot.
All visible roof slopes (photos 7, 8, 9) exhibit uniform hail-driven granule displacement with dark asphalt mat exposed—functional damage per HAAG standards requiring full slope replacement.
Hip and ridge cap shingles (photo 8) show directional granule bruising consistent with April 14, 2026 storm event.
Satellite dish mount on rear slope (photo 9) requires detach and reset during re-roof; mount penetrations need proper flashing upon reinstallation.
Pipe boot flanges visible in photo 9 show deformation consistent with hail impact—replacement required during re-roof.
Damage distribution is uniform across all orientations, confirming large-diameter hail event—spot repair is structurally and aesthetically infeasible.
Pipe boot flashing catastrophically fractured by hail impact — active leak risk confirmed (Photo 12)
B-vent galvanized pipe and flashing boot show hail impact indentations (Photo 13)
Abandoned satellite mount bracket on roof field creates unsealed penetrations requiring remediation (Photo 14)
Active Dish HD satellite requires detach-and-reset for re-roof; mount penetrations compromise shingle integrity (Photo 15)
Soffit and fascia at eave overhang show no visible storm damage (Photo 11)
Dozens of hail impacts per slope with circular granule displacement exposing dark asphalt mat — functional damage per HAAG standards from April 14, 2026 storm
Flat EPDM membrane on garage shows dozens of circular hail impact depressions requiring full membrane replacement
Ridge area exhibits hail strikes on both adjoining slopes confirming storm-wide damage pattern
Shingle edge detail reveals multiple shingle layers — re-roof condition prior to storm, no spot repair feasible
3-tab shingles with pervasive hail pattern cannot be matched or partially repaired; full replacement required
Aluminum gutter along eave displays circular hail dent deformation visible at multiple bracket points — soft metal confirms hail event.
Drip edge flashing shows impact indentations along the full run of the eave line.
Bay window aluminum trim and cap flashing are exposed soft metals susceptible to hail — visible deformation present.
Horizontal lap siding on west-facing elevation aligns with contractor-noted siding damage direction.
Shingle surface at eave edge shows granule loss and weathering compounded by storm impacts — non-repairable with current product.
Estimated roof age: ~12 years (installed circa 2014) — Shingle Type: Laminate/Architectural shingle

code_citation.jurisdiction: Iowa IRC 2015 (state adoption; home-rule cities may amend)

manufacturer_specs.requirement: 4-6 nails per shingle, ProStart starter required at eaves and rakes
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: GAF System Plus/Silver Pledge warranty VOID without proper starter and nailing pattern

code_citation.requirement: Existing roofing materials shall be removed before applying new roofing when existing roof has two or more applications of any type of roof covering
code_citation.supplement_argument: Complete tear-off is required per code for a code-compliant installation. Overlay is not code-compliant when damage necessitates deck inspection.
code_citation.title: Reroofing — Tear-Off Requirement

line_items.supplement_argument: Complete tear-off is required per code for a code-compliant installation. Overlay is not code-compliant when damage necessitates deck inspection.

code_citation.requirement: Roofing shall be applied in accordance with manufacturer's installation instructions. Manufacturer instructions carry the force of building code.
code_citation.supplement_argument: Per R905.1, manufacturer installation instructions are legally binding building code. All components specified by the manufacturer (starter, underlayment, flashing) are code-required, not optional upgrades.
code_citation.title: Manufacturer Installation Instructions = Law

line_items.supplement_argument: Ventilation required per IRC R806 — 1 SF NFVA per 150 SF attic area (1:150 ratio). OC warranty VOID without proper ventilation. Ridge vent must be R&R during re-roofing

code_citation.jurisdiction: Iowa IRC 2015 (state adoption; home-rule cities may amend)
code_citation.requirement: Step flashing shall be used where roof planes intersect vertical walls
code_citation.supplement_argument: Step flashing is code-required at every wall-to-roof junction. When roofing is replaced, step flashing must be replaced to maintain weathertight integrity per code.
code_citation.title: Step Flashing at Wall Junctions

line_items.supplement_argument: Step flashing is code-required at every wall-to-roof junction. When roofing is replaced, step flashing must be replaced to maintain weathertight integrity per code.

code_citation.jurisdiction: Iowa IRC 2015 (state adoption; home-rule cities may amend)
code_citation.requirement: Flashing required at chimney-to-roof intersections including step flashing, counter-flashing, and cricket/saddle where applicable
code_citation.supplement_argument: Chimney flashing is code-required at all chimney-roof intersections. Full replacement includes step flashing, counter-flashing, and apron. Reusing old chimney flashing on new roofing is a code violation.

line_items.supplement_argument: Chimney flashing is code-required at all chimney-roof intersections. Full replacement includes step flashing, counter-flashing, and apron. Reusing old chimney flashing on new roofing is a code violation.

code_citation.jurisdiction: Iowa IRC 2015 (state adoption; home-rule cities may amend)
code_citation.requirement: Flashing shall be installed at wall and roof intersections, at gutters, around roof openings, and at penetrations through the roof plane
code_citation.supplement_argument: Pipe jack flashing is code-required at every roof penetration. When roofing is replaced, all pipe boots must be replaced — reusing old pipe boots on new roofing is a code violation.
code_citation.title: Flashing — General (Pipe Penetrations)

line_items.supplement_argument: Pipe jack flashing is code-required at every roof penetration. When roofing is replaced, all pipe boots must be replaced — reusing old pipe boots on new roofing is a code violation.
8 hours roofer labor mandatory on every roofing estimate per industry standard
Siding labor minimum mandatory on every siding estimate

code_citation.jurisdiction: Iowa IRC 2015 (state adoption; home-rule cities may amend)

manufacturer_specs.requirement: CertainTeed-approved WRB required behind all siding. CertaWrap or equivalent with min 2" horizontal overlap, 6" vertical overlap. WRB must cover ALL sheathing before siding install.
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: SureStart PLUS warranty VOID without approved WRB behind siding

manufacturer_specs.requirement: WRB required — James Hardie-approved house wrap with min 2" shingle laps. Tyvek HomeWrap, DrainWrap, or equivalent. WRB continuity at corners is MANDATORY — cannot terminate at building corners.
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: ColorPlus warranty VOID without approved WRB. 30-year substrate warranty requires continuous WRB

manufacturer_specs.requirement: WRB required behind all vinyl siding per manufacturer specs. House wrap must be installed prior to siding. Cannot reuse existing WRB if damaged during siding removal.
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: Lifetime Limited warranty requires code-compliant WRB installation

manufacturer_specs.requirement: Install over sheathing BEFORE siding. Min 6" overlap at horizontal seams, 12" at vertical seams. Tape all seams with Tyvek tape. Must wrap around corners min 12".
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: Tyvek 10-year warranty VOID with improper installation

code_citation.requirement: WRB required on ALL exterior walls. Min 2" horizontal laps, 6" joint laps. Must wrap around corners per R703.1 — corner rule forces adjacent elevation if one elevation approved.
code_citation.supplement_argument: House wrap is mandatory per R703.2 on all exterior walls. The corner rule (R703.1) requires continuous weather barrier around corners — if the carrier approves siding on one elevation, the WRB must continue around the corner to the adjacent elevation to maintain continuity. This is not 'additional scope' — it is a code-required installation method.
code_citation.title: Weather-Resistive Barrier (House Wrap)

line_items.description: House wrap / Tyvek (code-required per IA-IRC R703.2)
line_items.supplement_argument: House wrap is mandatory per R703.2 on all exterior walls. The corner rule (R703.1) requires continuous weather barrier around corners — if the carrier approves siding on one elevation, the WRB must continue around the corner to the adjacent elevation to maintain continuity. This is not 'additional scope' — it is a code-required installation method.

manufacturer_specs.requirement: Head flashing required above all windows, doors, and penetrations. Kick-out flashing at roof-to-wall/gutter junctions. Z-flashing between dissimilar materials.
manufacturer_specs.warranty_text: Warranty VOID if moisture intrusion results from missing flashing

code_citation.requirement: Flashing required at ALL window and door openings per R703.4. Openings must be sealed to prevent water intrusion behind WRB. J-channel/window wraps are the method of compliance for vinyl and aluminum siding.
code_citation.supplement_argument: Window and door flashing is mandatory per R703.4. When siding is replaced, existing window wraps and J-channel are disturbed and must be replaced to maintain the water-resistant seal between the window frame and the weather-resistive barrier. This is not cosmetic — it is the primary defense against water infiltration at openings.
code_citation.title: Window & Door Flashing/Wraps

line_items.description: R&R Wrap wood window frame & trim with aluminum sheet
line_items.supplement_argument: Window and door flashing is mandatory per R703.4. When siding is replaced, existing window wraps and J-channel are disturbed and must be replaced to maintain the water-resistant seal between the window frame and the weather-resistive barrier. This is not cosmetic — it is the primary defense against water infiltration at openings.

photo_annotations.p03_02: Ridge vent and adjacent slopes show widespread hail bruising; dozens of circular depressions visible both faces.
photo_annotations.p03_03: Flat EPDM membrane on garage shows multiple circular hail impact marks; dozens of hits across full section.
photo_annotations.p04_01: Granule debris accumulated at drip edge and window head flashing confirms recent storm granule displacement.
photo_annotations.p04_02: B-vent chimney pipe shows hail impact indentations at joint collar; flashing boot deformed at base.
photo_annotations.p04_03: Rear slope shows widespread granule loss with dark mat exposure; ridge vent intact but shingles bruised.
photo_annotations.p05_01: Hip and ridge intersection shows hail-bruised cap shingles; ridge vent has directional granule loss.
photo_annotations.p05_02: Multiple slopes visible with uniform hail damage; pipe boots and satellite mount require detach/reset.
photo_annotations.p05_03: B-vent joint collar fractured from hail impact; sealant crumbled exposing gap—active leak risk.
photo_annotations.p06_01: Soffit and fascia intact; no visible hail or wind damage on eave overhang detail.
photo_annotations.p06_02: Hail impact fractured pipe boot collar — catastrophic failure, active leak point.
photo_annotations.p06_03: B-vent flashing boot shows impact indentations on galvanized pipe and base collar.
photo_annotations.p07_01: Abandoned satellite mount bracket creates unsealed penetrations; dozens of hail hits on field shingles.
photo_annotations.p07_02: Dish HD satellite on roof requires detach and reset; mount penetrations need sealing during re-roof.
photo_annotations.p07_03: Dozens of hail impacts with granule displacement exposing dark mat across full slope; functional damage confirmed.
photo_annotations.p08_01: Flat EPDM membrane on garage shows dozens of circular hail impact marks; full membrane replacement required.
photo_annotations.p08_02: Shingle edge at drip shows multiple shingle layers; hail-bruised mat visible at lifted tab edge.
photo_annotations.p08_03: Ridge vent area shows widespread hail strikes both slopes; granule displacement exposes bruised mat throughout.
photo_annotations.p09_01: Pervasive hail impacts across slope with circular depressions and embedded granules confirming storm damage.
photo_annotations.p09_02: Aluminum gutter and drip edge show hail dents along eave; bay window trim metal deformed from impacts.

photo_integrity.stamp: PHOTO INTEGRITY VERIFIED — 100% | 20 photos analyzed, 0 flagged for manipulation indicators | DumbRoof.AI Fraud Detection Engine
photo_integrity.summary: All 20 photos pass integrity analysis. The inspection documentation shows a residential property at 440 Christensen Rd, Waterloo, Iowa with consistent hail damage across all roof surfaces including asphalt shingles, flat EPDM membrane, and metal components. Damage patterns are uniform and consistent with a natural hail event — scattered granule displacement on shingles, circular impact marks on flat membrane, and general weathering of metal penetrations. No evidence of staged damage (no tool marks, BB gun impacts, or artificial gouging), no photo manipulation (lighting, shadows, and perspectives are consistent throughout), and no misrepresentation (all photos appear to be from the same property with consistent architectural features, shingle type/color, and surrounding landscape). The contract document appears authentic and properly executed.

config.user_notes: Shingle roof with rolled membrane flat roofing on garage, Siding damage on west side.

weather.storm_description: Searched 2026-04-14 ± 2 days for storm events near property. Near the subject property: 2.5" hail reported 3.2 mi from property. Hail reports in surrounding area: SPC Report — 3 E Otterville, IA (3.25"), SPC Report — 1 E Charles City, IA (3.25"), NEXRAD KDVN (3.0"), SPC Report — 1 ENE Waterloo, IA (2.5"), SPC Report — 3 W Toledo, IA (2.5"), NEXRAD KDMX (2.25"), SPC Report — Van Horne, IA (2.0"), NEXRAD KARX (1.75").
